title: Metastelma guanchezii
intro: Metastelma guanchezii, also known as Mexican climbing milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its climbing habit and showy, purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Metastelma guanchezii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Metastelma guanchezii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Metastelma guanchezii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Metastelma, which is a group of twining or climbing plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is known for its twining stems and funnel-shaped flowers, which are often purple or white in color. Metastelma guanchezii is native to Mexico, where it grows in rocky or sandy areas. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, it is closely related to other plants in the dogbane and oleander families.
